articles.title: Estate planning gap persists as concern fails to drive action
articles.description: More than half of American adults (56%) don’t have any estate planning documents — despite 73% saying it’s important. That’s according to a survey of 5,000 people conducted by Talker Research on behalf of Trust & Will. Results showed that, while the majority of adults (73%) say estate planning is important to them, just 26% actually have a will in place. And the levels of preparation don’t always correlate with age. Sandwiched between aging parents and their own kids, Gen X is actually the least protected generation in America.
